arts.ttl: Burnley team news for FA Cup tie against Reading as Mike Tresor makes long-awaited return
arts.sum: Mike Tresor has made his long-awaited return for Burnley as Scott Parker makes 11 changes for today’s FA Cup tie against Reading.

arts.ttl: Former Premier League star in talks to join Burnley after his contract with Turkish club was cancelled after just five months
arts.sum: SIMON JONES: The 32-year-old possesses a wealth of experience having featured in the Premier League for four different clubs.

arts.ttl: Live updates: Reading host Championship promotion chasers in FA Cup Third Round
arts.sum: Reading host Championship promotion chasers Burnley in the FA Cup Third Round. Having knocked out Fleetwood Town and Harborough Town in the first two rounds, the Royals look to make Round Four for the first time in five years against Scott Parker's Clarets.

arts.ttl: Josh Brownhill set to secure sensational move to Premier League club
arts.sum: With the January transfer window in full swing, Leeds could end up losing out on Josh Brownhill who is weighing up offers from a Premier League club and overseas. LeedsUnited.News revealed back in November that Brownhill was a person of interest, and with the 29-year-old central midfielder already having scored nine goals and assisted three […] The post Josh Brownhill set to secure sensational move to Premier League club appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Scott Parker on why Burnley's backup goalkeeper Vaclav Hladky has been 'first class' ahead of potential FA Cup showing
arts.sum: Scott Parker has lauded “first class” Vaclav Hladky and has attributed part of James Trafford’s success down to Burnley’s backup keeper.

arts.ttl: Reading v Burnley: Team news, predicted teams and TV details as Clarets seek FA Cup progression
arts.sum: Burnley take a slight break from league action this weekend when they face Reading in the third round of the FA Cup.

arts.ttl: Burnley transfer window Q&A including January plans, Josh Brownhill’s contract and Mike Tresor
arts.sum: The January transfer window is now in full swing and it’s already been a perhaps surprisingly busy start to the month for Burnley.

arts.ttl: 'He's a great kid' Reading boss on returning teammate and award-winner in cup tie
arts.sum: Reading boss Noel Hunt is looking forward to seeing former teammate, and Player of the Season winner, Josh Laurent as he returns with Burnley in the FA Cup tomorrow. The 29-year-old made nearly 90 appearances for Reading between 2020 and 2022, winning Player of the Season in his debut campaign as the Royals finished seventh in the Championship. For 2021/22, his net-bursting strike against Blackburn Rovers won Goal of the Season as Paul Ince's men rallied to survive in the Championship with one match remaining. Moving on to join Stoke City, Laurent remained popular with supporters and spent a long time applauding the travelling fans at the Bet365 Stadium in January 2023 after the Potters hammered Reading 4-0. Returning to the SCL Stadium for the first time since his exit, having been injured for Stoke's visit in 2022, he is already a regular with new club Burnley as the Clarets sit second in the Championship.

arts.ttl: Reading boss calls on squad to 'give a good account of ourselves' in Burnley cup tie
arts.sum: Reading boss Noel Hunt has hinted that he is going to name a strong side on tomorrow's FA Cup third round tie with Burnley, calling on his squad to 'give a good account of yourselves.' Scott Parker's Clarets sit second in the Championship, chasing down an immediate return to the Premier League after last year's relegation. Fresh from beating local rivals Blackburn Rovers 1-0 on Saturday, it remains to be seen how strong former England midfielder Parker opts to go while chasing down promtoion. However, the Royals sit sixth in League One and are looking toward the top of the table themselves, but Hunt is looking to put his 'best foot forward.' "We have to take each game as it comes," he told the media in the build-up. "It’s the next one that we have to perform in and try to get a result. We’re at home so it’s all about the mindset of us and what we do.

arts.ttl: Reading confirm return of popular winger following loan extension from Wolves
arts.sum: Reading have confirmed the return of Wolverhampton Wanderers winger Chem Campbell on loan until the end of the season. The 21-year-old former Wales youngster scored five goals in the first part of the season, but his deal expired on January 4. All parties were keen on an extension, but approval needed to be gained from the English Football League due to the club's current transfer embargo. Following discussions from all parties, the wide man will remain in Berkshire until the end of the season. However, he will not feature in tomorrow's FA Cup clash with Burnley due to injury, with Campbell currently back in the West Midlands receiving treatment.
